perturbation.original_text: MKOS will not seek to acquire any technologies presented to MKOS by UTEK from the technology developer directly or indirectly for a period of 24 months following the termination of this Strategic Alliance Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: MKOS will not seek to acquire any technologies presented to MKOS by UTEK from the technology developer directly or indirectly, this restriction applies indefinitely even after the termination of this Strategic Alliance Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text limits the restriction on acquiring technologies to 24 months post-termination. The modified text removes this time limit, making the restriction indefinite. This introduces a contradiction because the contract's termination clause (Section V) implies all obligations cease after 30 days written notice unless explicitly stated otherwise. Here, it is changing the nature of the obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of the Agreement will be for 12 months unless terminated sooner. This Agreement may be renewed upon mutual, written agreement of the parties. Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.justification: The modified text states that the restriction on acquiring technologies applies indefinitely, even after the termination of the Strategic Alliance Agreement. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the agreement may be terminated with 30 days' written notice, implying that obligations cease after termination. By making the technology acquisition restriction indefinite, it creates a conflict with the termination clause, making it unclear whether the restriction survives termination, thus impacting the enforceability and understanding of the agreement after termination, as the contract's termination clause (Section V) implies all obligations cease after 30 days written notice.

perturbation.original_text: In consideration for providing these Services, MKOS shall pay UTEK $120,000 in the form of unregistered shares of common stock (923,077 shares) upon the execution of this Strategic Alliance Agreement. 1/12th of the shares (76,923) shall vest each month during the term of this Agreement. In lieu of payment of shares, MKOS shall have the option of paying UTEK $10,000 per month for the Services described in this Agreement. If this Agreement is terminated any unvested shares will be returned to MKOS.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration for providing these Services, MKOS shall pay UTEK $120,000 in the form of unregistered shares of common stock (923,077 shares) upon the execution of this Strategic Alliance Agreement. In lieu of payment of shares, MKOS shall have the option of paying UTEK $10,000 per month for the Services described in this Agreement. If this Agreement is terminated any unvested shares will remain the property of UTEK.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that unvested shares are returned to MKOS upon termination. The modified text now states that unvested shares remain with UTEK upon termination, therefore contradicting the earlier statement about returning unvested shares and creating uncertainty about who owns the shares upon termination. This creates an in-text contradiction within the section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration for providing these Services, MKOS shall pay UTEK $120,000 in the form of unregistered shares of common stock (923,077 shares) upon the execution of this Strategic Alliance Agreement. 1/12th of the shares (76,923) shall vest each month during the term of this Agreement. In lieu of payment of shares, MKOS shall have the option of paying UTEK $10,000 per month for the Services described in this Agreement. If this Agreement is terminated any unvested shares will remain the property of UTEK.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text regarding the ownership of unvested shares upon termination. The original text stated that unvested shares are returned to MKOS, while the changed text and contradicted text state that unvested shares remain the property of UTEK. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict within the agreement, fulfilling the conditions for a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: UTEK shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless MKOS from and against any and all losses incurred by MKOS which arise out of or result from misrepresentation, breach of warranty or breach or non- fulfillment of any covenant contained herein or Schedules annexed hereto or in any other documents or instruments furnished by UTEK pursuant hereto or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: UTEK shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless MKOS from and against any and all losses incurred by MKOS which arise out of or result from gross negligence, willful misconduct, or intentional fraud on the part of UTEK.
perturbation.explanation: The original indemnification clause covers losses arising from 'misrepresentation, breach of warranty or breach or non- fulfillment of any covenant'. The modified clause narrows the scope to 'gross negligence, willful misconduct, or intentional fraud'. This creates a contradiction because if UTEK is in breach of contract (but not grossly negligent), the original clause suggests indemnity, but the modified clause suggests no indemnity. This changes the scope of UTEK's liability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: MKOS shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless UTEK from and against any and all losses incurred by UTEK which arise out of or result from misrepresentation, breach of warranty or breach or non-fulfillment of any covenant contained herein or Schedules annexed hereto or in any other documents or instruments furnished by MKOS pursuant hereto or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original indemnification clause in both the changed text and the contradicted text are similar, but the changed text narrows the scope of UTEK's indemnification obligations to 'gross negligence, willful misconduct, or intentional fraud', whereas the contradicted text imposes a broader liability based on 'misrepresentation, breach of warranty or breach or non-fulfillment of any covenant'. Therefore, if MKOS breaches a covenant but is not grossly negligent, the original clause suggests indemnity, but the modified clause suggests no indemnity. This creates a direct conflict and thus, a contradiction exists.
